title: PR body should follow the repo PR template, authored by the review stage
description:
## Problem

Harness-opened PRs don't reflect the repo's own PR template
(`.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`). The template only auto-fills the GitHub
web compose box; the harness opens PRs via the REST API
(`adw/workflow_runner.py::_notify_github` → `open_or_update_pr`) with an explicit
body, which bypasses the template. That body comes from
`adw/github.py::pr_body(story, outcome)`, which restates the ticket
(`## Outcome` + `**Ticket:** id — title` + the ticket *description* verbatim +
`Closes #n`). It has no access to what the stages actually did, so a reviewer
can't see *what changed / tradeoffs / risks* without reading the diff (see #50).

## Approach (decided)

Author the PR body in the **review stage**, not a hook. Review is the only stage
that has read the full diff and judged it against the criteria, so it is the
natural author of a human-facing change summary; a PreToolUse-style hook is the
wrong mechanism (it gates tool calls, can't intercept the urllib PR creation,
and has no view of the diff). Ride the existing status-block → `TicketOutcome` →
`_notify_github` seam.

This is an **enhancement with a fallback**, not a replacement: review emits the
description when it runs; `pr_body()` uses it when present and otherwise keeps
today's body.

### Accepted caveat (as-is, do NOT over-engineer)

Pipelines that skip review keep the current body: `STAGE_ORDER_BY_TYPE`
(`adw/workflow_runner.py`) has **bug skip review and chore skip plan+review**,
and `_notify_github` also fires on `blocked` outcomes where review didn't pass.
Those PRs keeping the plain ticket-description body is acceptable — no extra work
to special-case them.
acceptance_criteria: `stage_specs/review_feat.md` instructs the review stage to emit a
Pipelines that skip review (bug/chore) and non-done/blocked outcomes keep the
Unit tests cover: `pr_body()` with and without `pr_description`; a review
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
